content_snippet:
hostname rtr-edge-01
!
enable secret 5 $1$mERr$mARYP6ZUUmgYBRpfAhB2C/
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 description Uplink
 ip address 10.0.0.1 255.255.255.0
!
notes: Cisco IOS 'enable secret 5 $1$...' is the MD5-hashed enable password. Real corp engagement Red. Source: operator hkelley's posted rules in #78.

content_snippet:
hostname sw-floor3
!
enable password Cisco123!
!
line vty 0 4
 password admin
 login
!
notes: Cisco 'enable password' = cleartext fallback. Even worse than enable secret. Red.

content_snippet:
username admin privilege 15 password 7 094F471A1A0A12545C
username guest privilege 1 password 7 02050D480809
notes: Cisco 'password 7' = type-7 obfuscation, trivially reversible (Vigenere on fixed key). Recoverable to cleartext in seconds. Red.

content_snippet:
snmp-server community CORP_RW RW
snmp-server community public RO
snmp-server location DataCenter
notes: SNMP RW community grants write access to the device. Often the same string corp-wide. Red.

content_snippet:
snmp-server community DMZ_RO RO
snmp-server contact noc@corp.com
notes: SNMP RO grants read access — still useful for AD/network enumeration but lower tier than RW. Yellow per #78.

FileZilla SiteManager stores saved FTP/SFTP credentials. Pre-3.26 plaintext; modern versions XOR-obfuscated (trivially reversible). l0ss specifically called this out as a file Snaffler should find. Black.
FileZilla recent connections — hostnames and usernames, occasionally passwords. Yellow.
Generic 'credentials.xml' file. Snaffler operator (l0ss) called this out in #135 thread as a name Snaffler should match. Red.
ASP.NET Web.config <connectionStrings> with embedded SQL password. hkelley's #67 rules tracked this exact pattern. Red.
.NET appsettings.json with SQL password in connection string. Red — direct DB compromise vector.
Integrated Security=true means no password embedded — still surfaces DB server + DB name (good recon). Per hkelley's intent in #67: Yellow (no password = lower tier).
